description_long: This is a vintage, box-shaped television with a predominantly red exterior casing and a large, convex glass screen occupying most of the front face. The television features two long, metallic antennae extending from the top, characteristic of older analog models. Below the screen, there is a control panel with several buttons, dials, and a speaker grille, all set in a black section that contrasts with the red body. The sides and back of the television are solid red, with the rear panel displaying ventilation grilles, a power socket, and additional connection ports. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with rounded edges and a slightly slanted front panel. This television would likely be found in retro urban settings, such as old apartments, garages, or vintage shops.
description: A retro red television with a convex glass screen, black control panel, and dual metal antennae, featuring a robust, boxy design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex screen, black control panel with buttons and dials, and both antennae.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the solid red casing and one visible antenna, with no controls or screen visible.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing ventilation grilles, a power socket, connection ports, and the base of the antennae.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain red casing and one antenna, similar to the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black, plastic bezel with rounded corners and a matte finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Clustered on the bottom right of the front panel, arranged in a row with dials and buttons.
